title: Pan Seared Irish Pork Belly Skillet
description: A hearty Irish dish featuring crispy pork belly slices, smoked to perfection and served with a side of herbal flavors, perfect for a brunch mood and sugar-free friendly diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together smoked paprika, garlic powder, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork belly slices, making sure to coat them evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork belly slices for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are crispy and golden brown.
Remove the pork belly slices from the skillet and set them aside on a plate.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and potatoes to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes, or until they are tender.
Add the chicken broth to the skillet and stir to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the pork belly slices to the skillet and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pork belly is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
